title: Pleurothallis corniculata
intro: Pleurothallis corniculata, also known as the "corniculate Pleurothallis," is a species of orchid native to Central and South America, characterized by its unique, funnel-shaped flowers. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and fever, due to its reported antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Pleurothallis corniculata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs.
taxonomy: Pleurothallis corniculata is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Pleurothallis, which contains approximately 600 species of orchids. The species is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Within the Orchidaceae family, Pleurothallis corniculata is part of the subfamily Epidendroideae. This classification is based on its physical characteristics and evolutionary history.
